text:
I'll search for this paper to verify the exact title and authors.
The web search results consistently show that the paper exists with both Nicholas Mattei and Toby Walsh as authors. Multiple sources confirm this:

- 
The Springer Nature entry shows "Nicholas Mattei & Toby Walsh"

- 
The DBLP entry lists "Nicholas Mattei, Toby Walsh: PrefLib: A Library for Preferences"

- 
The PDF shows "N. Mattei and T. Walsh"


The automated checker's report that the paper was found in an academic database (with the Semantic Scholar CorpusID) confirms the paper exists. The discrepancy the checker noted (citing 2 authors vs. finding only "T. Walsh" in their metadata) appears to be a data completeness issue in the checker's extraction, not evidence of hallucination. The actual paper clearly has both authors.

The title matches exactly (with minor capitalization variation: "PrefLib: A Library for Preferences"), the authors are correct (Nicholas Mattei and Toby Walsh), the venue is confirmed as "Algorithmic Decision Theory" (ADT 2013), and the year is 2013.

EXPLANATION: The paper exists with the exact cited title and both cited authors confirmed across multiple academic sources and databases. The automated checker's author count mismatch reflects incomplete metadata extraction, not a hallucinated reference.
LINK: https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-3-642-41575-3_20
FOUND_TITLE: PrefLib: A Library for Preferences
FOUND_AUTHORS: Nicholas Mattei, Toby Walsh
FOUND_YEAR: 2013
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
